ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_00002383 (hamburger) is the closest parent. No specific code exists for a ham-and-rocket variant burger.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FAN (Hamburgers) is the closest parent category. No specific facet code for ham-and-rocket burger variant exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept exists for this specific burger variant.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant-style burger consisting of a beef patty, ham, rocket leaves, cheese, and condiments in a toasted bun, approximately 280 g.

nutrient_source.title: Hamburger, large, single patty, with condiments and vegetables — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 21110)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values adapted from USDA SR Legacy entry for a large single-patty hamburger with condiments and vegetables (NDB 21110 / FDC 174297). Ham component adds modest additional sodium and protein; rocket (arugula) contributes negligible macro change at typical serving quantities. Values represent a composite estimate for a ham-and-rocket burger of this type. No Singapore-specific HPB Foodo entry exists for this dish.

gi_source.title: Hamburger bun / mixed burger composite GI — University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS et al. (2021) International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values 2021, Am J Clin Nutr
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a rocket ham burger. GI of 54 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for a standard hamburger (white bun + beef patty composite), which reports GI approximately 51–57 (medium). The ham and rocket components do not materially alter the GI. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI x carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (54 x 62.7) / 100 = 33.9. Serving carbohydrate: 22.4 g per 100 g x 280 g serving = 62.7 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~520 mg per 100 g; ~1456 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this burger due to its high sodium content from processed ham, cheese, and condiments, which can significantly contribute to daily sodium limits of 2000 mg recommended by the Singapore Health Promotion Board.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium GI (54) with high glycemic load per serving (~34) due to refined white bun
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume this burger only occasionally and in reduced portions, as the refined-flour bun produces a medium glycemic index and a high glycemic load per standard serving, which can cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~4.8 g per 100 g), trans fat (~0.4 g per 100 g), and high sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption of this burger due to its combined saturated fat, trans fat, and high sodium content, all of which are associated with elevated LDL cholesterol and increased cardiovascular risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Rocket Ham Burger is a Western-style beef burger served at Harry's Bar and Grill in Singapore (Boat Quay outlet). It consists of a grilled beef patty topped with sliced ham, fresh rocket leaves (arugula), cheese, and condiments, all assembled in a toasted burger bun. The rocket leaves contribute a peppery, slightly bitter flavour that contrasts with the savoury richness of the ham and beef patty. The burger is a substantial meal item priced at approximately SGD 34.77, positioning it as a premium casual-dining burger. Nutritionally, it is energy-dense at approximately 266 kcal per 100 g, with moderate protein (~13.8 g/100 g) and fat (~13.5 g/100 g), and a meaningful carbohydrate load from the bun (~22.4 g/100 g). Sodium content is high (~520 mg/100 g) due to the processed ham, cheese, and condiments, making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index is estimated at 54 (medium), driven primarily by the refined white bun, and the glycemic load per standard serving is high (~34), which is clinically relevant for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The burger is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those following a halal diet without specific confirmation from the restaurant. Allergens include gluten, dairy, egg, and soy.
common_user_queries: What is a Rocket Ham Burger?
How many calories in a Rocket Ham Burger?
Is the Rocket Ham Burger healthy?
What is the sodium content of a ham burger?
Can diabetics eat a Rocket Ham Burger?
What is rocket in a burger?

confidence_reason: No exact HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA Branded Food entry exists for this specific restaurant burger. Nutrient values are adapted from the USDA SR Legacy large hamburger with condiments and vegetables entry, which is the closest approved-source analogue. The ham and rocket components introduce minor adjustments to sodium and micronutrients. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database hamburger analogue. Confidence is medium rather than low because the USDA SR Legacy source is a strong structural match for this burger category.
notes: Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entry used for this record. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, potassium, calcium, iron, cholesterol, vitamin C, vitamin A, B-vitamins, vitamin E, phosphorus, magnesium, zinc, selenium, and water values are drawn from the same USDA SR Legacy source (FDC 174297) and represent the best available approved-source estimate for this burger category. The food is served exclusively at Harry's Bar and Grill, Boat Quay, Singapore, as recorded in GrabFood Singapore data. Price SGD 34.77 is preserved from the input record. Halal status is not confirmed; consumers should verify with the restaurant. The rocket (arugula) topping is a distinguishing feature of this burger variant compared to a standard hamburger.

q1_food_identity.notes: food_name is the precise canonical English name 'Rocket Ham Burger'. This is a Western burger dish; no mandatory non-English local-language names are required under G6 multilingual rules (rules apply to Singaporean, Japanese, Korean, Indonesian, Thai, Vietnamese dishes — this is a Western dish served in Singapore). Q1 passes.

q2_core_macro_completeness_and_source_in.notes: energy_kcal (266.0), protein_g (13.8), fat_g (13.5), carbohydrate_g (22.4) all present, numeric, and supported by USDA SR Legacy FDC 174297 (large hamburger with condiments and vegetables). nutrient_source fully populated. Q2 passes.

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: dietary_fibre_g (1.3), sodium_mg (520.0), saturated_fat_g (4.8), sugar_g (4.2) all present as numeric values supported by USDA SR Legacy source. Q3 passes.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index (54, numeric > 0), gi_category ('medium'), glycemic_load_per_serving (33.9), gi_source fully populated referencing University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 peer-reviewed journal. Analogous estimate basis documented. Q4 passes.

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: FoodOn FOODON_00002383 (hamburger, closest_parent) and FoodEx2 A0FAN (Hamburgers, closest_parent) both present and defensible. SNOMED CT is null with gap note. No ontology codes invented. Q5 passes.

q6_llm_ready_semantics.notes: natural_language_description is well over 200 characters, explains what the food is, preparation method, taste/texture, cultural context (Harry's Singapore), clinical relevance (hypertension, diabetes, CVD), and key nutrients. Q6 passes.

q7_health_context.notes: All three required conditions present: Hypertension (high concern, sodium), Type 2 Diabetes (high concern, GI/GL), Cardiovascular Disease (high concern, saturated fat/trans fat/sodium). Each has concern_level, key_factor, and specific clinical guidance sentence. human_condition_name for type2_diabetes is 'Type 2 Diabetes'. Q7 passes.

q8_aliases_and_multilingual_synonym_cove.notes: aliases list contains 6 entries covering canonical name, alternate English names, and restaurant-specific name. aliases_by_language english array matches flat aliases exactly. This is a Western dish; no mandatory non-English language aliases are required. All strings in aliases_by_language appear verbatim in flat aliases. Q8 passes.

veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: PASS — All six hard gates pass and all eight Q criteria score 1/1 for a total of 8/8. The Rocket Ham Burger is a Western-cuisine burger served at Harry's Boat Quay Singapore. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy FDC 174297 (large hamburger with condiments and vegetables), the closest approved-source analogue. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database hamburger analogue (GI 54, medium). No mandatory non-English local-language names are required for a Western dish. Ontology anchors FoodOn FOODON_00002383 and FoodEx2 A0FAN are defensible closest-parent matches. Health context covers Hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, and Cardiovascular Disease with specific clinical guidance. The vitamin_d_mcg sentinel value of -1 is documented in notes. Aliases are consistent between aliases_by_language and flat aliases.
